title: Retrieval Review: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences
summary: Retrieval Review: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences retrieves prior skills in an original changed zoom input and system-preference constraints scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css zoom reflow, css visible hidden content, css focus visibility, css user preferences, css touch targets, css accessibility regression in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed zoom input and system-preference constraints artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Volunteer Shift Deck accessibility hardening here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the accessible CSS regression system here. */

options.text: Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 1: a desktop layout can remain fixed because browser zoom simply scales the screenshot.

Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 1: test complete workflows at high zoom and narrow effective widths, then remove fixed dimensions, clipping, and nonwrapping assumptions that lose content.
Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 1: disable browser zoom with viewport metadata so the desktop layout remains stable.

options.text: Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 3: a subtle color shift is enough focus evidence for every control.

options.text: Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 5: small icons are usable when their SVG graphic is visually sharp.

Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 5: make icons eight pixels wide and add a tooltip for users who miss them.
